steps.title: Identify the dimension scale factor
steps.content: Both dimensions increase by 20%, so each new dimension equals 120% of the original: $1.2 \times \text{original}$. The dimension scale factor is $k = 1.2$.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule
steps.content:
When all linear dimensions scale by factor $k$, area scales by $k^2$. Since $k = 1.2$:

$$(1.2)^2 = 1.44$$

The area will be multiplied by 1.44.

steps.title: Calculate the new area
steps.content: Multiply the original area by the scale factor: $$3,200 \times 1.44 = 4,608 \text{ square inches}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
